Title: Critical regulation of galactolipid synthesis controls membrane differentiation and remodeling in distinct plant organs and following environmental changes

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane lipid remodeling under phosphate starvation; supports thylakoid lipid precursor import from ER to plastids
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Permease-like protein; putative ABC transporter component
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for incorporation of ER-derived lipid precursors into plastid galactolipids; mutation causes accumulation of triacylglycerols and trigalactolipids and reduced PA import into plastids
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant characterization (tgd1); lipid incorporation analysis; PA accumulation analysis; subcellular localization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TGD1 was identified through characterization of tgd1 mutants defective in use of ER-derived lipid precursors for plastid lipid synthesis. 2. TGD1 encodes a permease-like inner envelope protein proposed to function in an ABC-type phosphatidic acid transporter. 3. Loss of TGD1 reduces precursor import into plastids and alters galactolipid-related lipid remodeling.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in DAG/PA precursor supply rather than direct galactolipid catalysis.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Chloroplast and extraplastidic membranes under phosphate starvation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plastid membrane lipid precursor transport for galactolipid biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PA-binding protein with predicted mycobacterial cell entry domain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports transfer of ER-derived phosphatidic acid/lipid precursors into plastids for galactolipid synthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TGD2 was identified from tgd mutants affecting plastid use of ER-derived precursors. 2. TGD2 binds phosphatidic acid and likely participates with TGD1/TGD3 in precursor transport. 3. Defects alter thylakoid lipid precursor incorporation and galactolipid remodeling.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Common gene for lipid transport, not direct synthase.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Chloroplast and extraplastidic membranes under phosphate starvation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports ATP-dependent plastid lipid precursor transport linked to galactolipid formation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for efficient ER-to-plastid lipid precursor transfer affecting galactolipid biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant characterization (tgd3); ATPase activity assay; localization analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TGD3 was identified in the tgd mutant series affecting plastid lipid trafficking. 2. TGD3 has basal ATPase activity and localizes to the inner envelope system. 3. It likely energizes transport of lipid precursors used for galactolipid biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of TGD lipid transport system.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Chloroplast and extraplastidic membranes under phosphate starvation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for lipid transport between ER and plastids contributing to galactolipid synthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for lipid transport between the endoplasmic reticulum and plastids, thereby influencing galactolipid precursor supply
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TGD4 is reported as essential for lipid transport between ER and plastids. 2. This transport supports plastid galactolipid biosynthesis. 3. It acts in precursor trafficking rather than direct catalysis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Limited mechanistic detail in this review excerpt.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane lipid remodeling under phosphate starvation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to phosphate starvation-induced DGDG accumulation by hydrolyzing phosphatidylcholine to provide DAG; knockout reduces about half of DGDG accumulation under Pi starvation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mutant analysis (npc5); lipid remodeling analysis under phosphate starvation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NPC5 is induced by phosphate starvation and participates in membrane lipid remodeling. 2. npc5 mutants show nearly 50% reduction of DGDG accumulation under phosphate starvation. 3. NPC5 partially supplies DAG for galactolipid synthesis from phosphatidylcholine.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Other pathways including PLD plus PAP may compensate.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and other membranes under phosphate starvation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports eukaryotic-pathway contribution to galactolipid biosynthesis, especially under phosphate starvation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper eukaryotic-pathway contribution to galactolipid biosynthesis; disruption significantly affects galactolipid synthesis especially under phosphate starvation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant disruption analysis; biochemical identification as PAP
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtPAH1 encodes a soluble extraplastidic phosphatidate phosphohydrolase. 2. It contributes to DAG supply for the eukaryotic pathway of galactolipid biosynthesis. 3. Disruption affects galactolipid biosynthesis most strongly under phosphate starvation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions with AtPAH2; acts in precursor generation rather than terminal galactolipid assembly.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports eukaryotic-pathway contribution to galactolipid biosynthesis, especially under phosphate starvation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for proper eukaryotic-pathway contribution to galactolipid biosynthesis; disruption significantly affects galactolipid synthesis especially under phosphate starvation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant disruption analysis; biochemical identification as PAP
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtPAH2 encodes a soluble extraplastidic phosphatidate phosphohydrolase. 2. It contributes to DAG supply for the eukaryotic pathway of galactolipid biosynthesis. 3. Disruption affects galactolipid biosynthesis most strongly under phosphate starvation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from UDP-galactose to diacylglycerol to produce MGDG
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid envelope; predicted plastid-targeted in source species
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes MGDG synthesis; expression in E. coli causes strong accumulation of MGDG and cell division defect
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Partial purification; internal peptide sequencing; cDNA cloning; recombinant expression in E. coli; enzyme assay; lipid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMGD was the first plant MGDG synthase molecularly identified. 2. Recombinant CsMGD expressed in E. coli showed MGD activity and caused accumulation of MGDG to ~20% of total lipids. 3. CsMGD established the enzymatic basis of plastid MGDG synthesis and enabled cloning of orthologs.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced upon illumination in cotyledons; responsive to cytokinin
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light; cytokinin; redox state; phosphatidic acid; phosphatidylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Has modest sequence similarity to bacterial MurG; supports evolutionary link to bacterial glycosyltransferases.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major membrane lipid for thylakoid biogenesis and membrane formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for bulk MGDG synthesis in greening tissues not formally established as sole rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for galactolipid production and chloroplast membrane formation, but overexpression/stress benefits remain untested
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: E. coli expression caused cell division defect

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from UDP-galactose to DAG to form monogalactosyldiacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT28 glycosyltransferase; transit peptide-containing Type A MGD
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast inner envelope membrane; faces intermembrane space
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in photosynthetic organs; induced by light and cytokinin; increased after wounding in shoots or drought/salt stress in roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major structural lipid of thylakoid membranes; required for chloroplast development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major isoform mediating bulk MGDG synthesis in photosynthetic tissues; loss causes defective thylakoid membranes, impaired photosynthesis, dwarf transparent seedlings, and embryo/cotyledon maturation defects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockdown mutant analysis; recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli; GFP localization; isoform-specific immunoblotting; expression analyses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMGD1 is the unique Type A MGD in Arabidopsis and the predominant MGDG synthase in photosynthetic organs. 2. Knockdown causes severe chloroplast-development and seedling defects due to lack of thylakoid membrane formation. 3. Its expression and activity are promoted by light/cytokinin and its enzyme activity is redox- and phospholipid-responsive.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for bulk MGDG synthesis in photosynthetic organs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in photosynthetic organs, induced by light and cytokinin
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light; cytokinin; redox state; phosphatidic acid; phosphatidylglycerol; wounding; drought; salt
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance chloroplast membrane biogenesis, but stress-resistance effects of overexpression remain unknown
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockdown causes severe growth and photosynthetic penalties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highest Vmax and DAG affinity among Arabidopsis MGD isoforms; can utilize both prokaryotic and eukaryotic DAG pools.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the target product and supplies substrate for DGDG synthesis during phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from UDP-galactose to DAG to form MGDG
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT28 glycosyltransferase; Type B MGD lacking transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in flowers and roots; induced under phosphate limitation; GUS expression in pollen tubes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports membrane lipid remodeling under phosphate starvation and reproductive/non-photosynthetic organ lipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to phosphate starvation-induced DGDG accumulation and non-photosynthetic organ galactolipid synthesis; single mutant shows no obvious phenotype but contributes in double mutant with mgd3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli; GFP localization; mutant analysis including double knockout; GUS promoter analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMGD2 is a Type B outer-envelope MGD preferentially using eukaryotic DAG species. 2. It is induced by phosphate starvation and expressed in flowers/roots, supporting DGDG synthesis in non-photosynthetic tissues. 3. Single knockout has little visible phenotype, but combined loss with MGD3 compromises lipid remodeling.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key conditional control step under phosphate limitation in conjunction with MGD3
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in flowers and roots; induced under Pi-limited condition
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate starvation; auxin; cytokinin; phosphite; hydrogen peroxide
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering phosphate-starvation lipid remodeling in roots and non-photosynthetic organs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single mutant has no strong phenotype; redundancy with MGD3 and compensation by MGD1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots; extraplastidic membranes after conversion to DGDG
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely supplies MGDG directly for subsequent DGDG synthesis rather than large MGDG accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms MGDG that is used for DGDG synthesis during phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from UDP-galactose to DAG to form MGDG
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT28 glycosyltransferase; Type B MGD lacking transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots and young seedlings; induced under phosphate limitation; GUS expression in pollen tubes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Critical for phosphate starvation-induced membrane lipid remodeling, especially in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Main Type B MGD responsible for DGDG increase under phosphate starvation; knockout reduces root DGDG synthesis and causes slower growth under Pi limitation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli; mutant analysis (mgd3-1 and double mutants); GFP localization; GUS promoter analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMGD3 is a phosphate starvation-inducible Type B MGD highly expressed in roots and young seedlings. 2. mgd3 mutants show reduced DGDG accumulation, especially in roots, and slower growth under phosphate limitation. 3. Together with MGD2, it is indispensable for membrane lipid remodeling in roots during phosphate starvation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major conditional control step for phosphate starvation-induced DGDG increase in roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots and young seedlings; induced under Pi-limited condition
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate starvation; auxin; cytokinin; phosphite; hydrogen peroxide
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for phosphate-use adaptation through membrane lipid remodeling in roots
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes slower growth under phosphate limitation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots; extraplastidic membranes after conversion to DGDG
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression may also reflect cell division activity and phosphate demand.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream step converting MGDG to DGDG
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a galactose residue to MGDG using UDP-galactose to form DGDG
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in photosynthetic organs; induced by drought and under nitrogen- or phosphate-limited conditions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Lamellar membrane lipid important for thylakoid stability and phosphate-starvation membrane remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major DGDG synthase for bulk production; mutation causes pale green dwarf plants with reduced photosynthetic activity and slower growth under high temperature and phosphate limitation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant isolation (dgd1); map-based cloning; coexpression in E. coli with CsMGD1; lipid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGD1 was the first DGDG synthase identified and is responsible for bulk DGDG synthesis in Arabidopsis. 2. dgd1 retains only ~10% of wild-type DGDG and accumulates oligogalactolipids, indicating additional processive galactosyltransferase activity exists. 3. DGD1 is constitutive but stress inducible, contributing to DGDG increases under phosphate limitation, drought, and nitrogen limitation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for bulk DGDG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in photosynthetic organs; induced by drought stress and under N- or Pi-limited growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate starvation; nitrogen limitation; drought; high temperature
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for improving membrane stability and phosphate-saving lipid remodeling, but severe growth/photosynthesis defects caution against loss of function
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes pale green dwarf phenotype and photosynthetic defects
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Thylakoid and extraplastidic membranes under phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DGDG:MGDG ratio affects thylakoid membrane stability; dgd1 suppressor dgs1-1 linked to MGD2/3 induction by H2O2.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream step converting MGDG to DGDG
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a galactose residue to MGDG using UDP-galactose to form DGDG
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT4 glycosyltransferase lacking N-terminal transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Normally low expression; induced under nitrogen- or phosphate-limited conditions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Conditional DGDG synthesis during nutrient stress and membrane remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Second DGDG synthase with stress-inducible expression; single mutant has no obvious phenotype but enhances dgd1 defects in double mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant expression in E. coli and activity assay; mutant analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGD2 is a second Arabidopsis DGDG synthase identified by similarity to DGD1 and confirmed biochemically. 2. It localizes to the outer envelope and functions distinctly from DGD1 because of very different expression patterns. 3. DGD2 contributes conditionally under phosphate or nitrogen limitation and in double-mutant backgrounds.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Normally low expression, induced under N- or Pi-limited growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential complementary target with DGD1 for nutrient-stress membrane remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single knockout has no visible phenotype due to redundancy
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Extraplastidic membranes under phosphate starvation; plastid membranes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinct N-terminus suggests different envelope targeting mechanism from DGD1.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional induction by light and cytokinin; post-translational redox activation; lipid-mediated activation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light, red light, cytokinin, thioredoxin/redox status, phosphatidic acid, phosphatidylglycerol
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major switch controlling bulk MGDG production in green tissues
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Thylakoid membrane formation and photosynthetic function
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Signal-responsive control of AtMGD1 abundance/activity strongly influences MGDG accumulation and chloroplast development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analyses under light/cytokinin; in vitro redox and lipid activation assays; mutant analyses
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMGD1 expression is cooperatively induced by light and cytokinin in photosynthetic tissues. 2. Its enzymatic activity is stimulated by reducing conditions/thioredoxin and by phosphatidic acid or phosphatidylglycerol. 3. Thus AtMGD1 integrates developmental and biochemical signals to control MGDG production.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included here only because the review emphasizes its regulatory control; not a classical TF/signaling regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Hormone- and phosphate-responsive enzymatic control points
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional induction under phosphate starvation; activation requires auxin and is antagonized by cytokinin; suppressed by phosphite
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Phosphate starvation-induced membrane lipid remodeling and DGDG synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Phosphate starvation, auxin, cytokinin, phosphite, hydrogen peroxide
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential conditional controllers of DGDG accumulation in roots under phosphate limitation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phosphate-saving substitution of phospholipids in cellular membranes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hormonal and phosphate-sensing regulation of Type B MGD expression determines root DGDG accumulation during phosphate starvation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GUS reporter assays; hormone/inhibitor treatments; mutant analyses including mgd3 and mgd2mgd3; phosphite treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Phosphate starvation activates Type B MGD expression and consequent DGDG accumulation. 2. Auxin signaling is required for full induction, whereas cytokinin antagonizes this response. 3. Phosphite suppresses Type B MGD induction independently of auxin, indicating phosphate-sensing regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate starvation; auxin; cytokinin; phosphite; hydrogen peroxide
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not classical regulatory genes; grouped here only because the paper focuses on regulation of their expression.
